what_it_does: Defines a static landscape decision-card mockup with a left question/context panel and right-side selectable option cards.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono, plus shared piece and phone stylesheets.
Sets a fixed 844x390 landscape screen using a flex split layout.
Left panel presents a DECISION label, the title "Merge Strategy", a prompt, and contextual explanatory text.
Right panel contains two horizontal option cards: "Absorb" and "Fork".
The "Absorb" option is marked recommended and shows a recommended tag plus visible choose action.
Recommended and selected states share gold border, goldwash background, emphasized order marker, tag styling, and visible choose button.
Includes hover styling for option cards and loads /surface/runtime/organisms.js at the end.
standouts: Uses a side-by-side decision layout rather than stacking prompt and options vertically.
The recommended state is expressed through multiple coordinated signals: tag, border, background, order marker, and choose control.
Option cards include compact impact metadata in a monospace line.
conventions: Uses project design tokens for spacing, radius, type sizes, colors, and borders.
Uses semantic-ish class prefixes tied to the decision-card component area.
Keeps component-specific CSS inline in the HTML file.
Uses fixed artboard sizing and absolute presentation context through the surrounding view structure.
concerns: The layout is tightly coupled to a fixed 844x390 landscape canvas.
External font loading and /surface shared assets are required for the file to render as intended.
Selected-state CSS exists, but no local script or markup demonstrates changing selection state.
notes: This file is a landscape decision-card concept showing context on the left and two selectable paths on the right.
questions: Should the selected state be interactive in this file or only represented as available styling?
Is the fixed 844x390 canvas meant as an artboard constraint or a reusable component size?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated landscape concept matches the file: it renders a question/context panel on the left and option paths on the right.
intention_signals: It seems intended to test a compact landscape decision interaction where explanatory context stays visible while the user compares two possible paths.
novelty: A fixed landscape card composed as a two-panel decision surface with option cards that combine recommendation, action, and impact metadata.
contribution: A landscape split-panel decision pattern with contextual framing on one side and selectable alternatives on the other, including visual treatment for recommended and selected states.
summary_for_embedding: A static HTML/CSS landscape decision-card mockup with a left context panel and right-side option cards for choosing between Absorb and Fork.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for exploring a landscape decision UI where the prompt, context, comparison options, recommendation signal, and action affordance are visible together.
